mnemonics.title: File Access Methods
mnemonics.content: SAND: Sequential (S), Indexed (A), Direct (N),/Direct (D) - Remember: 'Some Indexes Need Direct Access'

mnemonics.title: Storage Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: FAST: Fast (Register), A (Cache), S (RAM), T (Tape/Disk) - 'Fast Access Short-term Storage'

mnemonics.title: Normalization Forms
mnemonics.content: KISS: Keys (1NF), Individual (2NF), Separate (3NF), Separate (BCNF) - 'Keep It Structurally Sound'

acronyms.usage: Maintains cluster chain information for files in file systems like MS-DOS, Windows
Storage technology combining multiple disks for performance and fault tolerance
Standard language for managing relational databases
Operations involving reading from or writing to storage devices
Set of functions for file and storage operations in applications

visualTips.visualization: Draw three columns: Sequential (line of boxes), Indexed (main data with side index table), Direct (hash function → bucket)
visualTips.purpose: Visualize how each method locates data differently

visualTips.visualization: Pyramid with registers at top (smallest, fastest), then cache, RAM, SSD, HDD, Tape at bottom (largest, slowest)
Stack of blocks: 1NF (atomic values), 2NF (no partial dependency), 3NF (no transitive dependency), BCNF (determinant must be key)

analogies.analogy: Like reading a book from page 1 to find a specific page - you must go through previous pages.
Like using a book index to find a topic, then turning to the relevant pages.
Like going directly to a row number in a spreadsheet using the row number.
Like a delivery truck consolidating multiple small packages into one trip rather than making many trips.
Like organizing a wardrobe: shirts with shirts, pants with pants (normalized) vs. all mixed together (denormalized).
